Defines the built-in properties and methods for the ECMA String.prototype object.

# Purpose
The code defines the built-in properties and methods for the `String.prototype` object in a JavaScript engine, specifically for the ECMAScript standard. It uses macros to declare object identifiers, object properties, number properties, and routine properties associated with the `String.prototype`. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `String.prototype`, while the `OBJECT_VALUE` and `NUMBER_VALUE` macros define properties such as the constructor and length, respectively. The `ROUTINE` macro specifies various string manipulation methods, including `toString`, `valueOf`, `concat`, `slice`, `indexOf`, and others, each linked to their corresponding C function implementations.

The code also includes conditional compilation directives to include or exclude certain methods based on configuration flags, such as `CONFIG_DISABLE_REGEXP_BUILTIN` and `CONFIG_DISABLE_ANNEXB_BUILTIN`. These flags control the inclusion of regular expression-related methods like `match`, `replace`, `search`, and `split`, as well as the `substr` method. The use of macros and conditional compilation allows for flexible configuration and extension of the `String.prototype` functionality within the JavaScript engine.
